content: I just got back from a work trip to Seattle last week. The coffee there is seriously next level.
Seattle's coffee scene is legendary! Did you get to visit any of the famous spots like Pike Place Market?
Yeah, I hit up the original Starbucks and a few local roasters. The weather was surprisingly nice too - sunny most days which apparently never happens.
That's amazing! I've heard Seattle can be quite rainy. What brought you there for work?
I'm a data scientist at BioGenesis Pharma and we were doing some training at our Seattle office. It was my first time visiting that location.
That sounds like a great opportunity! Do you travel often for work?
Not usually, this was pretty special. Most of my projects are remote these days. I did squeeze in some mountain biking at Duthie Hill though - highly recommend it if you're ever there.
Mountain biking in Seattle sounds incredible! I'll definitely keep that in mind. What else did you do in your free time?
Well, I'm getting married next year so I dragged my fiancé to look at a venue we'd been eyeing. It was perfect but way out of our budget. Back to the drawing board on that front.
Wedding planning can be so stressful! I hope you find the perfect place that fits your budget. Did you try any good restaurants while you were there?
We went to Canlis for a fancy dinner - absolutely worth it. But honestly, I was most excited about visiting the Museum of Pop Culture. I'm a huge sci-fi nerd and their exhibits were incredible.
The MoPOP is fantastic! I love how Seattle embraces its geeky side. Did you pick up any souvenirs?
Just some local coffee beans and a leather journal from a craftsman at Pike Place. I've been getting into leatherworking as a hobby lately, so I couldn't resist.
That's such a cool hobby! I'd love to see what you make. Did the conference or training cover anything particularly interesting?
Actually yes - they had a session on AI applications in pharmaceutical research that blew my mind. I've been experimenting with some of the techniques in my sourdough baking too, weirdly enough.
Wait, how are you applying AI to sourdough baking? That sounds fascinating!

I built a little model to optimize fermentation times based on temperature and humidity. My starter's never been happier. Though my coworkers might prefer I stuck to data science at work instead of bringing my baking experiments there.
That's brilliant! You're combining your professional skills with your passion. I'd say that's a win-win. Any other highlights from your trip?

Just reconnecting with some college friends who moved there. We played this new board game they'd picked up called 'Cascadia' - it's all about building ecosystems in the Pacific Northwest. Kind of perfect for Seattle.
That sounds like the perfect souvenir from your trip! Board games are such a great way to stay connected with friends. Did you play anything else while you were there?
Just some classics like Catan and Ticket to Ride. But Cascadia was definitely the standout. I might need to pick it up for game nights with my fiancé and our friends back home.
Game nights are the best! Especially when you're planning a wedding and need some stress relief. I hope you had a wonderful trip overall despite the venue disappointment.
